items.summary: Leah Stewart was swimming at Coogee Beach when she was attacked just before 11am on Saturday.

items.title: Fuel excise rebate call next week as Anthony Albanese says ceasefire does not mean 'return to normal'
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government will give Australians "appropriate notice" about the fuel excise rebate, as it continues to monitor the issue.

items.title: Police charge two men after improvised gun discoveries in SA
items.summary: South Australian police have separately arrested two men for the alleged possession of a handgun disguised as a mobile phone, and a homemade "pen gun".

items.title: NT government introduces new air quality testing plan, including measuring carcinogenic emissions
items.summary: The Northern Territory government has announced a plan to introduce new air quality monitoring of toxic pollutants, including any carcinogenic emissions. It follows a review that found gas giant Inpex had underestimated its emissions in Darwin.

items.title: Former AFL footballer Nick Stevens jailed over $170k Mildura pool fraud
items.summary: An ex-footballer has been locked up after a jury found him guilty of defrauding multiple families with his regional pool business.

items.title: Victoria Police investigate off-field brawl during Ararat and Lismore-Derrinallum football match
items.summary: Victoria Police is investigating an off-field fight at a local football match in Ararat over the weekend, sparking concerns about "dreadful" behaviour at grassroots sporting events.

items.title: South Australian community, family reacts to Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal
items.summary: The family and community of Socceroos star Nestory Irankunda are "very emotional" after his goal-scoring World Cup debut. His cousin says he will be "forever grateful" for the support the family received after arriving in Australia, and "their blessing is seeing this at the big stage".

items.title: Police investigate dozens of dirt bike riders in Newcastle after Bad Apples 'riot' in Dangar Park
items.summary: Newcastle residents and authorities condemn a clothing company stunt that degenerated into a "riot" involving dozens of dirt bike riders.

items.title: 'Fakeaway' recipes to feed the family
items.summary: Ordering in of an evening can become a costly exercise but these five tasty recipes are sure to hit the spot.

items.title: Lebanon and nuclear weapons: The stumbling blocks in Trump's Iran negotiation
items.summary: The US-Iran deal faces five volatile days as questions remain over nuclear weapons and Israel's attacks on Lebanon.

items.title: Why Cameron Munster is always Queensland's State of Origin saviour
items.summary: Ever since his first State of Origin match, Queensland has looked to Cameron Munster in times of trouble. Now, with the series on the line at the MCG, his state will ask its greatest modern hero to do so again.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Curaçao's special goal and De Oranje brighten up day four
items.summary: De Oranje hit the World Cup and in the middle of a 7-1 thumping, Curaçao's lone goal might have meant the most of all. Here are today's quick hits.

items.title: Ipswich man Bobby Weaver sentenced to life in jail after murdering neighbour and hiding remains in freezer
items.summary: Justice Paul Smith says Bobby Weaver murdered his friend, 58-year-old David Thornton, "in cold blood".

items.title: Vacant and underused land to make way for housing in next phase of Queensland land activation program
items.summary: Three developers will take ownership of 6 hectares of state-owned land in Banyo in Brisbane's north and build townhouses, affordable housing and an aged care facility.

items.title: Tasmanian man allegedly caught drink driving three times in just over a week
items.summary: A Tasmanian man has been charged after being allegedly caught drink driving for the third time in just over a week, with him having to be cut from his car in one instance.

items.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter given another reprieve, with workers keeping their jobs for now
items.summary: The administrator for the mothballed manganese smelter is continuing to engage with the consortium designated as its preferred buyer, the ABC understands.

items.title: Perth girl Hania Ahmed remembered after fatal police shooting in Pakistan
items.summary: A nine-year-old girl fatally shot by police who were pursuing armed robbers in Pakistan is remembered as happy and bubbly by the principal of her Perth school, as her family tries to deal with their "tragic loss".

items.title: Magnitude-2.9 earthquake felt near large dam in NSW Snowy Mountains
items.summary: The magnitude-2.9 earthquake was felt in parts of the Snowy Mountains just before 1pm on Sunday, according to Geosceince Australia.

items.title: Bureau of Meteorology's only SA wave buoy offline for third time in two years
items.summary: The wave buoy off Kangaroo Island is the only such device operated by the Bureau of Meteorology in South Australia and is relied upon by professional divers and fishers.

items.title: Before Donald Trump's Iran deal, he and Benjamin Netanyahu were pursuing separate agendas
items.summary: As Donald Trump was trying to push his Iran deal over the line, Benjamin Netanyahu bombed Beirut, showing once again the Israeli prime minister has other priorities.

items.title: World Cup: FIFA's hydration break farce the ultimate insult for weary football fans
items.summary: Football fans are forced to endure much to keep their love of the beautiful game alive, but FIFA's cynical new "hydration breaks" are stretching the friendship like never before.

items.title: Musician and online personality Oliver Tree presumed dead after helicopter crash
items.summary: Ahead of his Australian tour, the American musician and online personality is reportedly one of six passengers involved in a fatal helicopter collision.

items.title: China's ability to hit Australia with missiles 'real and growing', Lowy Institute says
items.summary: New analysis warns that China's military is developing a "real and growing" ability to hit the Australian mainland with missiles, and can already threaten Australia's trade routes, subsea cables and critical infrastructure.

items.title: Police treating deaths of man and girl in bay at Concord as murder-suicide
items.summary: Investigators are trying to determine what led to a suspected murder-suicide on Saturday, after the bodies of a father and young child were recovered from a bay off Concord.

items.title: Picturesque islands in WA's north set to become transport hubs after $20m sale
items.summary: A Kimberley iron ore mine with idyllic views across a tropical sea will close after 70 years of operation as a new phase begins for offshore oil and gas.

items.title: A lost Sam Pang went looking for something. He found comedy
items.summary: This workplace satire starring Sam Pang has Tasmania's first ever AFL team firmly in its sights.

items.title: WA needle exchanges raise concerns over 'dramatic' rise in injectable peptide use
items.summary: Needle exchanges in Western Australia are reporting dramatic increases in people accessing their services for peptide use.

items.title: Australians are dying from syphilis, a disease that was almost eliminated more than a decade ago
items.summary: Forty-two babies in Australia died from congenital syphilis between 2011 and 2025, with the outbreak driving health workers into communities to raise awareness of the old-world disease.

items.title: Plans to open Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran ceasefire reached — as it happened
items.summary: Pakistan, which has been negotiating peace talks between the US and Iran, says a deal to end the war has been agreed.

items.title: Alex de Minaur claims runner-up in Rosmalen Championships after tie-break loss in final
items.summary: Australia's top-ranked men's player Alex de Minaur fights to a tie-break in the Rosmalen Championships final but falls to his Polish opponent.

items.title: World Cup: Socceroos hero Nestory Irankunda hits back as Türkiye star says his team 'dominated'
items.summary: Socceroos goalscoring hero Nestory Irankunda says Türkiye could not back up its talk, despite one of its stars claiming post-game Australia had been "dominated".

items.title: US and Iran announce ceasefire and plans to reopen Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The United States and Iran reach an agreement to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz, offering relief to the global economy after three months of fighting.

items.title: A health worker holding an inject that cures syphilis
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: 'Potential explosive hazards' at Shackleton and Scott's historically important huts in Antarctica
items.summary: Chemicals found inside two of Antarctica's most important historical sites, Earnest Shackleton's and Robert Falcon Scott's huts, could pose "potential explosive hazards", a report has warns.

items.title: Woman falls to her death after rope jump accident in Brazil
items.summary: In videos of the accident circulating online, two men hoist the 21-year-old above their heads and launch her off a bridge in São Paulo.

items.title: Property Council hits back at claim tax debate was 'submerged by vested interests' — as it happened
items.summary: Housing groups have addressed the Senate inquiry into the federal government’s sweeping changes to negative gearing and capital gains.

items.title: Northern Grampians Shire Council ignores community calls for CCTV at Stawell crime 'hotspot'
items.summary: Parents are working to petition a west Victorian council to install CCTV cameras at a skate park that police are calling a crime "hotspot". The council says taking on a public safety role is "unreasonable".

items.title: Cooper Connolly hits stunning century to hand Australia victory over Bangladesh in third ODI
items.summary: Cooper Connolly fights off his body going into shock due to cramps to record his maiden international century.

items.title: Gold Coast nurse charged over alleged offences involving six teenage girls
items.summary: Detectives charged the 58-year-old man following investigations into the alleged indecent treatment of children between November 2020 and May 2024.

items.title: PFAS queries continue as Wagga residents reassured drinking water is safe
items.summary: Wagga Wagga has two known sites contaminated by "forever chemicals" and some say not enough information about how it is being managed is available.

items.title: Iran weekly briefing: In the build-up to the ceasefire, Trump resumed dropping f-bombs
items.summary: Donald Trump is reported to unleash another expletive-laden outburst at Benjamin Netanyahu over Israeli strikes as the US and Iran finalise a peace deal.

items.title: World Cup: Pat Beach's Socceroos dream is Mat Ryan's nightmare, and Mark Schwarzer can relate to both
items.summary: Pat Beach became a surprise national hero with his performance against Türkiye, while incumbent goalkeeper Mat Ryan watched from the bench. Mark Schwarzer has experienced both sides of that coin.

items.title: Concerns over Great Artesian Basin water impacts in new BHP agreement
items.summary: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.

items.title: World Cup: Socceroo Nestory Irankunda knew how he would celebrate before even taking the field
items.summary: Nestory Irankunda's childhood hero is none other than Tim Cahill. So it was fitting that more than 20 years after Cahill's famous celebration, this generation's Socceroos hero paid tribute.

items.title: Calls to make it easier to break lease after woman sexually assaulted in northern Tasmania
items.summary: Annie feared for her safety from a new next-door neighbour in northern Tasmania but was told she couldn't break her lease without continuing to pay rent. She was then sexually assaulted.

items.title: Swiss voters set to reject population cap in referendum
items.summary: A projection shows voters prioritising economic stability and the country's ties with the European Union over immigration concerns.

items.title: AI company Firmus to become Tasmania's biggest power user if three planned sites come to fruition
items.summary: Artificial intelligence company Firmus Technologies will become Tasmania's biggest power user if its ambitions to establish three "AI factories" in the state come to fruition.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Cowboy country turns into football epicentre in Dallas, Texas
items.summary: Japan and Netherlands fans are turning out in big numbers in Dallas, Texas, as the city embraces its status as the main World Cup hub.

items.title: Japan to roll out nationwide LGBTQIA+ education for the first time
items.summary: The country is taking a big step in its efforts to address gay rights but not everyone is convinced it goes far enough.

items.title: Business groups call on parliament to 'reject' CGT changes as inquiry kicks off
items.summary: Business groups are urging the government to scrap its capital gains tax changes as the first tranche of measures is scrutinised at a parliamentary inquiry.

items.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'
items.summary: Almost $15 billion has been spent over a decade via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of "black cladding".

items.title: Palestinians fear 'archaeology has become a tool' in Israel's plans in the West Bank
items.summary: Palestinians believe there is more to Israel's recently announced plans to develop heritage sites along the West Bank, arguing archaeology has "become a tool, a mechanism, to justify settlement and justify the displacement of Palestinians" across the area.

items.title: Dashdot encouraged customers to proceed days before collapse as liquidators examine insolvency timeline
items.summary: Emails and messages seen by ABC News show Dashdot's staff were still encouraging prospective customers to engage with the buyers agency shortly before it entered voluntary liquidation.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX trillions are fuelled by AI and his pied piper power over investors
items.summary: To get a sense of the difference between the Tesla and SpaceX IPOs, consider that 1.7 billion seconds ago was 1972, when Elton John released Rocket Man, while 1.7 trillion seconds ago was 52,000BC, when we shared the planet with Neanderthals.

items.title: Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days just weeks before he died
items.summary: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

items.title: What to watch in June, from homegrown romance to remote mystery
items.summary: Plus Brisbane is a main character in a new rom-com, Russell T Davies delivers a devastating drama, and we bring you more shows lighting up TV this month.

items.title: This week in history quiz, June 15: Test yourself on these past events
items.summary: When was Salt Lake City awarded the XIX Winter Olympics? And how much do you know about the Fast and the Furious films? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

items.title: Race Around The World recap: From Jamaica to Greenland, catch the week two films
items.summary: From dating in Jamaica to world domination in Greenland, catch up on the films from the second week of the new season of Race Around The World.

items.title: Sweden's Premier League stars shine to cast Tunisia aside
items.summary: Sweden dispatches Tunisia after Côte d'Ivoire scores a last-minute winner against Ecuador and Japan finds a late leveller against the Netherlands.

items.title: British forces intercept Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker in the English Channel
items.summary: The British armed forces intercept a sanctioned oil tanker in a bid to disrupt the oil revenue ​that helps fund Russia's war in Ukraine.

items.title: Socceroos boast heavy Adelaide connection — here's how SA turned out to support them
items.summary: Thousands of South Australian soccer fans descended on The Drive in Adelaide city to watch Adelaide's own Nestory Irankunda score the first half-opener, leading to Australia's win over Türkiye on Sunday.

items.title: Melbourne fans overjoyed as Socceroos scored against Türkiye in World Cup
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Jubilant fans pack Federation Square to celebrate Socceroos win over Türkiye
items.summary: In Melbourne, Federation Square became the epicentre of fan festivities, with capacity crowds packing into the CBD venue for a Socceroos viewing party.

items.title: World Cup: Socceroos' great gamble pays off as Patrick Beach vindicates Tony Popovic's big call
items.summary: With his first World Cup team selection, Tony Popovic produced one of the biggest shocks in Socceroos' history. After 90 minutes against Türkiye, his gamble was vindicated.
